architecture.note: pythia-12b's official config.json carries vocab_size 50688 (padded vs the 50304 of the rest of the suite); we record what the official config states.

training_tokens.as_published: 299,892,736,000 tokens = 143,000 steps x 2,097,152-token batch; 'just under 1 epoch' on the (non-deduplicated) Pile

ac_config_adaptations: GELU MLP -> SwiGLU encoding: AC's dense FFN schema/loader accepts only swiglu (3-matrix, 3*d*ffn params). Pythia-12B's true FFN is a 2-matrix GELU MLP (2*d*20480 params). Verbatim swiglu encoding would inflate AC's parameter ledger ~24%. We encode the param-equivalent width ffn_dim = round(2/3 * 20480) = 13653, so 3*5120*13653 = 209,710,080 ~ 2*5120*20480 = 209,715,200. AC ledger total: 11,843,850,240 vs published 11,846,072,320 (-0.02%). The architecture_residual MLP/attn-ratio term has weight 0.0 in stock constants, so the narrower ffn_dim does not move shape penalties.
rotary_pct 0.25 not representable in AC's schema; config records rope with base 10000. No AC quality term reads rotary_pct.

parallelism.tensor_parallel = 1: the param-equivalent ffn_dim 13653 = 3^2x37x41 has no power-of-2 divisor, and AC requires TP | ffn_dim. TP affects only throughput metrics; the quality prediction (this study's target) is TP-invariant.
metadata.params declares AC-ledger-consistent values (11.84B); published counts recorded above.

prediction_inputs.note: 'Just under 1 epoch' -> repetition ~0, unique tokens left null (D_eff = D). Evaluation context == pretraining context so context_utility is identically zero.
